title: Docs: Lowercase cgroup
body:
We have some cases where cgroups are called CGroups. From a quick `git grep`:

```
docs/source/markdown/options/cgroups.md:Determines whether the container creates CGroups.
docs/source/markdown/options/cgroups.md:The **disabled** option forces the container to not create CGroups, and thus conflicts with CGroup options (**--cgroupns** and **--cgroup-parent**).
docs/source/markdown/options/cgroups.md:The **no-conmon** option disables a new CGroup only for the **conmon** process.
docs/source/markdown/options/cgroups.md:The **split** option splits the current CGroup in two sub-cgroups: one for conmon and one for the container payload. It is not possible to set **--cgroup-parent** with **split**.
docs/source/markdown/podman-stats.1.md.in:Note:  Podman stats does not work in rootless environments that use CGroups V1.
docs/source/markdown/podman-stats.1.md.in:Podman stats relies on CGroup information for statistics, and CGroup v1 is not
docs/source/markdown/podman-stats.1.md.in:Note: Rootless environments that use CGroups V2 are not able to report statistics
```

These should all be changed to the correct spelling, `cgroup`.
